keyEvents.impact: Loss of imperial domains; lingering ambition to reclaim regional leadership
Radical Westernizing reforms; secular-Islamist fault line defines politics ever since
Anchored to the West as the alliance's southeastern bastion against the Soviets
Willingness to use force unilaterally for perceived national interests despite Western censure
Army as self-appointed guardian of the state; cycles of intervention and civilian pushback

keyEvents.event: EU accession talks open then stall
keyEvents.impact: Disillusionment with Europe; pivot toward independent regional power role
Mass purges of military and bureaucracy; deepened distrust of Western allies
Erdogan concentrates power; personalized, transactional foreign policy

history.narrative: A swing power straddling NATO membership and a transactional relationship with Russia, extracting concessions from both sides while answering to neither. Neo-Ottoman ambitions drive military reach into Syria, Libya, and the Caucasus, a booming drone industry underwrites strategic independence, and the Kurdish question remains the non-negotiable core of its security doctrine.

redLines.description: Establishment of a Kurdish autonomous entity along Turkey's southern border
Challenge to Turkish sovereignty claims in the Aegean and Cyprus
Foreign support for groups Ankara designates as coup plotters or PKK affiliates

goals.description: Establish Turkey as the indispensable regional hegemon of the Middle East and Eastern Mediterranean
Suppress Kurdish separatism at home and prevent PKK-linked entities abroad
Balance between NATO and Russia to maximize leverage over both
Build a fully independent defense industry led by drone and missile exports
Tame inflation and stabilize the lira to sustain regional ambitions
